name: Grandma's Chicken and Dumpling Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 can 10 3/4 oz. cream of chicken soup
1 can 10 3/4 oz. cream of mushroom soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the fryers, water, bouillon cubes, peppercorns, and cloves in a kettle. Bring to a boil.
2. Reduce heat and simmer until the chicken is tender (about 1 1/2 hours).
3. Cool the chicken slightly, then cut into bite-sized pieces and set aside.
4. Strain and skim the chicken broth.
5. Add the reserved chicken and broth to a large kettle along with the cans of broth, cream of chicken soup, cream of mushroom soup, celery, carrots, onion, potatoes, bay leaf, peas, and seasoned salt.
6. Place the cover on the kettle and simmer the soup on low heat for about 2-3 hours.
7. About 30 minutes before serving, mix the feather dumplings by sifting together the dry ingredients.
8. Add the egg, melted butter, and enough milk to make a moist, stiff batter to the mixture.
9. Drop spoonfuls of the batter into the boiling liquid.
10. Cook, covered without peeking, for 18-20 minutes or until the dumplings are done.
Yield: 10 - 12 servings.
